summary: The best index for measuring the burden of disease is Disability Adjusted Life Years (DALYs), which comprehensively considers both the extent and quality of health impacts across populations. This makes it superior to alternative metrics that focus solely on mortality or specific morbidities without incorporating broader measures of population impact. \(\boxed{\text{D}}\)
